summary: Cathy O'Neil announces her participation in the FATML (Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency in Machine Learning) workshop at NIPS in Montreal. She will be on a closing panel discussing real-world examples where fairness and accountability in data-driven decisions were relevant. Upon returning, she plans to join the Eric Garner protest at Washington Square Park, connecting her academic work on algorithmic fairness to street-level activism against police violence.
key_points: FATML workshop at NIPS conference addresses fairness, accountability, and transparency in machine learning
Panel discussion will focus on real-world examples where data-driven decisions raised fairness concerns
O'Neil draws an implicit connection between algorithmic accountability and civil rights activism
The timing juxtaposes academic discourse on fairness with grassroots protests against systemic injustice
money_quotes: On the panel, we will be discussing examples of data driven projects and decisions where fairness, accountability, and transparency came into play, or should have.
key_insight: Academic work on algorithmic fairness and street activism against police violence are connected struggles for accountability and justice.
